findings.rendered_text_content: Perplexity rendered a product-side error toast: 'i seuredeu-neun jonjaehaji anseumnida' (this thread does not exist). The chat area returned to the initial landing state showing 'mueoseutideun mureoboseyo' (ask anything) placeholder. No streaming response body appeared in the chat UI. No 'boan gyeonggo' warning rendered.
findings.note_on_eventstream: Chrome DevTools EventStream tab showed 0 parsed rows despite the response Content-Type being text/event-stream. The 4.9 kB response body was NOT parseable as SSE by Chrome's strict parser. Response body could not be retrieved for forensics ('Failed to load response data. Request with the provided ID has already finished loading' — Chrome streaming-fetch body-retention limitation).
likely_root_cause_candidates: (a) Perplexity product-side session/thread-ID error triggered by unauthenticated or anonymous session (no prior thread created; perplexity_ask POST with bogus thread_id rejected by backend with 200-JSON-error-in-SSE-wrapper)
(b) APF intercepted and replaced the 875B envelope, but perplexity's client parser saw the envelope and emitted thread-not-found error toast instead of rendering warning text

(c) The 4.9 kB body was larger than the expected 875B envelope — if this is the actual APF envelope after templating expansion, the size fits a captured-verbatim T1c candidate; if this is perplexity's own error JSON, APF was not active on this path

status_detail: FAIL: expected_text 'boan gyeonggo' NOT rendered. Fourth distinct behavior pattern observed this cycle: product-side error toast (not silent-hang like wrtn 547, not full-answer like you/mistral/baidu scenarios). APF interception indeterminate from this run alone — the evidence points weakly toward either (a) perplexity's own thread-init failure on anonymous session, or (b) APF envelope schema mismatch with perplexity client parser. To disambiguate: (1) run a benign prompt via run-scenario to capture perplexity's successful SSE event schema, (2) compare services=127 perplexity_sse 875B envelope to the captured events, (3) apply T1c captured-verbatim fix if schema diverges. This is the first service this cycle that confirmed an actual text/event-stream endpoint exists (vs wrtn which has no SSE at all), so it's a promising T1c target.
errors: product-side error 'thread does not exist' toast rendered; no APF warning envelope rendered; check-warning expected_text not found
